train_3968.llm_answer: Consumer advocate : there is no doubt that the government is responsible for the increased cost of gasoline, because the government's policies have significantly increased consumer demand for fuel, and consumer demand for gasoline cannot increase without causing gasoline prices to rise steadily.

train_316.llm_answer: Statement1: To suit the needs of corporate clients, advertising agencies have successfully modified a strategy originally developed for political campaigns. train_4399.llm_answer: Consumer magazine: because front-loading washers use less water than top-loading washers, they are less effective at cleaning clothes. So, to get clothes really clean in a front-loading machine you need to use a washer that uses more water.

train_2130.llm_answer: The dean of computing must be respected by the academic staff and be competent to oversee the use of computers on campus. The only deans whom academics respect are those who hold doctoral degrees, and only someone who really knows about computers can competently oversee the use of computers on campus. Furthermore, the board of trustees has decided that the dean of computing must be selected from among this university's staff. Therefore, the dean of computing must be a professor who holds a doctoral degree in computer science.
train_2130.llm_explanation: The modification ensures that Premise 2 becomes necessary by directly linking the requirement of "really knowing about computers" to holding a doctoral degree in computer science. Premise 1, which specifies that only computer science department staff with doctoral degrees know about computers, is no longer necessary because the Argument now relies on the broader condition in Premise 2 that any professor with a doctoral degree in computer science meets the competency requirement.

train_1423.llm_answer: Consumer advocate : ephedra is a naturally occurring compound that some people use as a weight-loss aid. Recently, the government prohibited the sale of dietary supplements containing ephedra on the grounds that ephedra has been shown to have grave side effects. This prohibition is unreasonable. Echinacea is another natural compound that has been shown to have side effects, yet echinacea is widely available at health food stores, just as ephedra was before the prohibition.
train_1423.llm_explanation: By adding the phrase "just as ephedra was before the prohibition," the Argument now relies on Premise 2 (ephedra was available before the prohibition) as a necessary assumption to argue that the prohibition is unreasonable. train_594.llm_answer: Statement1: Often patients with ankle fractures that are stable, and thus do not require surgery, are given follow-up x-rays because their orthopedists are concerned about possibly having misjudged the stability of the fracture. Statement2: When a number of follow-up x-rays were reviewed, however, all the fractures that had initially been judged stable were found to have healed correctly, regardless of the orthopedist's experience. Statement3: Therefore, it is a waste of money to order follow-up x-rays of ankle fractures initially judged stable.
train_594.llm_explanation: The modification to Statement2 indicates that the healing outcomes were consistent regardless of the orthopedist's experience, which supports Premise2's claim that general practitioners are less reliable in judging stability. This makes Premise2 relevant and strengthens the argument, while Premise1, which discusses the review of x-rays from various orthopedists, does not contribute to the argument's conclusion.

train_3979.llm_answer: Statement1: More and more law firms specializing in corporate taxes are paid on a contingency-fee basis. Statement2: Under this arrangement, if a case is won, the firm usually receives more than it would have received if it had been paid on the alternate hourly rate basis. If the case is lost, the firm receives nothing. Statement3: Most firms are likely to make more under the contingency-fee arrangement because they take on fewer cases, allowing them to focus on high-value, high-success-rate cases.
train_3979.llm_explanation: By modifying Statement3 to emphasize that firms take on fewer cases under the contingency-fee arrangement, Premise2 (which states that such firms take in fewer cases) directly strengthens the Argument. train_4104.llm_answer: Statement1: It is popularly believed that a poem has whatever meaning is assigned to it by the reader. Statement2: But objective evaluation of poetry is possible only if this popular belief is false; for the aesthetic value of a poem can be discussed only if two readers can agree on the meaning of the poem.

train_1182.llm_answer: Statement1: Critic : rock music is musically bankrupt and socially destructive, but at least the album covers of rock lps from the 1960s and 1970s often featured innovative visual art. train_2941.llm_answer: Music critic : fewer and fewer musicians are studying classical music, decreasing the likelihood that those with real aptitude for such music will be performing it. train_2941.llm_explanation: Premise 2 weakens the Argument by suggesting that audiences can enjoy classical music even with less-than-ideal performers, implying that encouraging more musicians may not be necessary. train_939.llm_answer: Statement1: Twelve years ago and again five years ago, there were extended periods when the darfir republic's currency, the pundra, was weak: its value was unusually low relative to the world's most stable currencies. Both times a weak pundra made darfir's manufactured products a bargain on world markets, and darfir's exports were up substantially. Statement2: Now some politicians are saying that, in order to cause another similarly sized increase in exports, the government should allow the pundra to become weak again, despite the fact that darfir's manufacturing sector is now operating at near-peak levels.
train_939.llm_explanation: The modification clarifies that the current operational capacity of darfir's manufacturing sector does not weaken the argument, while the efficiency improvement in manufacturing (implied by Premise 2) suggests that exports could increase without weakening the currency, thus weakening the argument for allowing the pundra to weaken again.

train_1248.llm_answer: Educator : some experimental educational programs, based on the principle that children's first education should take place at home, instruct parents in how to be their child's" first teacher. train_4022.llm_answer: Super express shipping company has implemented a new distribution system that can get almost every package to its destination the day after it is sent. train_2437.llm_answer: The photographs that the store developed were quite unsatisfactory. The customer claims to have handled the film correctly. train_4467.llm_answer: Statement1: Naturalist : sent2: the recent claims that the tasmanian tiger is not extinct are false. train_390.llm_answer: Statement1: Tax reformer: the proposed tax reform legislation is being criticized by political groups on the right for being too specific and by political groups on the left for being too vague. train_168.llm_answer: Statement1: If one wants to succeed, then one should act as though one were genuinely confident about one's abilities, even if one actually distrusts one's skills. train_2814.llm_answer: Statement1: A company has developed a new sensing device that, according to the company's claims, detects weak, ultralow-frequency electromagnetic signals associated with a beating heart. train_2710.llm_answer: Virginia and her brother William disagree over when their father was born: Virginia claims it was in 1935 and William claims it was in 1933. The hospital where their father was born has no records for 1933 but has complete records for 1935 -- records that do not include a birth record for their father. Therefore, he must have been born on a day and month not recorded in 1935.
train_2710.llm_explanation: By shifting the conclusion to focus on the day and month of birth rather than the year, Premise 2 (which states that Virginia and William know the day and month of their father's birth) becomes a necessary assumption for the argument. train_3939.llm_answer: Human skin gives off an array of gaseous substances, including carbon dioxide and lactic acid, both of which attract mosquitoes. However, neither of these two substances, whether alone or combined with one another, will attract mosquitoes as much as a bare human arm will during the day, where a mosquito has no visual cues. train_2662.llm_answer: Statement1: Downtown petropolis boasted over 100 large buildings 5 years ago. Statement2: Since then, 60 of those buildings have been demolished. train_548.llm_answer: Statement1: Acme's bank loan must be immediately repaid in full if acme's earnings fall below $1 million per year. train_97.llm_answer: Art critic : nowadays, museum visitors seldom pause to look at a work of art for even a full minute. They look, perhaps take a snapshot, and move on. train_2815.llm_answer: Critic : people today place an especially high value on respect for others ; yet, in their comedy acts, many of today's most popular comedians display blatant disrespect for others. train_3551.llm_answer: Statement1: Politician : a major social problem is children hurting other children.
